repository.name: Gym Anything for Robotics
repository.description: Physics-first embodied task environments for Isaac Sim, with inspectable evidence from first principles to full robot execution.

environments.title: LaundryFold
environments.summary: A solved released towel fold plus five real panel-and-seam garment constructions with accepted topology-safe reset states.
environments.description: LaundryFold has two scoped evidence tracks: a finite-mass clamp and passive-spin roller causally complete the released P6 towel half-fold, while a cotton half-sleeve tee, button-down shirt, five-pocket denim jeans, loop-pile towel, and asymmetric draped top retain their actual cut panels, seams, openings, pockets, facings, and trims. P13 accepts all five garment-specific flat lays with zero degenerates and exact crossings and binds 15 reviewed Isaac front/back/construction views to those states. P12 separately verifies a topology-safe T-shirt release and matched IPC stress. Only P6 is an end-to-end manipulation; all materials, seams, contacts, and fixtures remain uncalibrated engineering priors.

status.label: Towel task + five garment resets pass
status.detail: P6 is a solved numerical towel manipulation; P9--P13 add five construction, task, topology, and accepted-reset cases, not five solved robot folds.

profiles.label: Interactive isolated solver
profiles.description: Open the Isaac visual/sensor frontend backed by the isolated Newton 1.4 worker; use manual fixtures or toggle the complete P6 reference controller.

profiles.label: Inspect five garments
profiles.description: Open the P13-bound visual inspector and switch among five real constructions plus front-owned, back-owned, and all-panel views.

controls.action: Select garment / cycle front, back, construction view
Print garment scene audit / close inspector
Reference policy, pause, fresh reset, or cycle camera

environments.title: AppleCut
environments.summary: A robot-driven knife cuts a volumetric apple with explicit fracture, contact, and energy accounting.
environments.description: AppleCut couples a Franka-held knife to a GPU deformable apple and measures the complete cut as a physics experiment: interface separation, fracture work, contact reaction, element quality, vibration, and energy closure are all recorded rather than inferred from animation.

status.label: Execution proven
status.detail: The whole apple separates in the coupled robot run; the separate whole-cut energy-acceptance gate remains open.

profiles.label: Robot + full apple
profiles.description: Launch the embodied keyboard-controlled cutting scene.

profiles.label: FEM coupon
profiles.description: Open the smaller fracture coupon for fast mechanism inspection.

environments.title: Humanoid Football
environments.summary: A floating-base G1 causally kicks a regulation-scale ball through shoe impact, spin, aerodynamics, pitch, and a continuous whole-ball goal test.
environments.description: Humanoid Football treats an atomic penalty kick as a coupled physics experiment. Bounded robot commands create a real shoe-ball impulse and outgoing spin; a six-DoF ball then evolves under pressure-shell contact, compliant grass, Reynolds-dependent drag, Magnus, coherent low-spin wake, frame/net contact, and IFAB whole-ball scoring. Two profiles pass internally, while named-specimen calibration remains explicit and open.

status.label: Two causal profiles pass
status.detail: Curl and low-spin runs both score and recover with zero outgoing ball-state assignments; ball, pitch, net, and exact G1 calibration remain open.

profiles.label: Causal curl goal
profiles.description: Run the canonical visible G1 strike whose contact-generated sidespin bends the ground shot into goal.

profiles.label: Low-spin goal
profiles.description: Run the contact-generated low-spin profile with a deterministic coherent wake; this is not yet a calibrated high-speed knuckleball.

controls.action: Runs the finite 10 s kick, goal, and recovery audit
Orbit and inspect the Isaac viewport while the rollout runs
Choose curl or low-spin before launch

environments.title: LEGO Assembly
environments.summary: A Franka grasps, aligns, presses, and pulls real-scale bricks through a calibrated stud-and-tube clutch.
environments.description: The LEGO environment begins with independent rigid bricks and explicit connector contact. Its evidence separates the high-resolution calibration fixture from the interactive embodied task and audits reach, grasp, alignment, insertion, seating, retention, and pull-off without treating a visual snap as success.

status.label: Assembly + extraction proof
status.detail: A fresh policy run seats blue, then extracts it 11.128 mm relative to fixture-retained red with zero red motion; calibration to measured physical bricks remains an explicit open gate.

profiles.label: Interactive assembly
profiles.description: Launch the full Franka brick-assembly task.

environments.title: Lock & Key
environments.summary: Insert and turn the correct cut key in a five-pin professional padlock; wrong keys bind physically.
environments.description: A real-scale padlock scene combines a recognizable laminated lock body and key set with the verified pin-stack mechanism underneath. Correct-key opening, wrong-key binding, ward refusal, insertion, turning, and shackle release are exposed as separate evidence rather than hidden in a scripted unlock event.

status.label: Mechanism verified
status.detail: The five-pin discrimination, physical insertion, turning, and shackle presentation passes are retained.

profiles.label: Padlock bench
profiles.description: Launch the complete interactive lock-and-key scene.

environments.title: PenCap Click
environments.summary: Push and pull a cap through a compliant pen snap while measuring the force-displacement click.
environments.description: PenCap models the tiny interference and compliance that create a cap's tactile click. It includes a direct force-control mechanism scene and an embodied dual-Franka workspace, with traces for insertion, retention, release, cap lift, and the remaining calibration gaps.

status.label: Snap-fit verified
status.detail: The simulated click and embodied manipulation work; force calibration and tolerance sensitivity remain open.

profiles.label: Snap mechanism
profiles.description: Directly push and pull the cap through the snap fit.

profiles.label: Dual-arm workcell
profiles.description: Manipulate pen and cap with either Franka arm.

environments.title: Titration Lab
environments.summary: A particle burette dispenses calibrated aliquots into a laboratory titration with measured mixing and endpoint logic.
environments.description: Titration Lab joins particle-based fluid mechanics to a separately tested chemistry layer. The retained scene audits volume per particle, leakage, droplet formation, drainage, mixing, endpoint crossing, and repeatability across the complete laboratory procedure.

status.label: Bench gates passed
status.detail: The physical bench and full emergent titration pass their retained gates; droplet-scale fidelity is documented separately.

profiles.label: Live titration bench
profiles.description: Run twelve dispense cycles, then hold the live state for inspection.

controls.action: Orbit and inspect the retained live state
Cycles three evidence cameras every 40 s

environments.title: SortHub
environments.summary: Scan labeled parcels, route them through a live conveyor cell, and sort with a humanoid station operator.
environments.description: SortHub reconstructs an industrial facility around a measurable end-to-end pipeline: optical reads, moving parcels, controlled routing, reachable robot interaction, and destination placement. Its acceptance record distinguishes the verified routing chain from the harder transfer questions around general grasping.

status.label: End-to-end verified
status.detail: Fresh acceptance reports 12/12 barcode reads and 12/12 sorts with no missort.

profiles.label: Live routing cell
profiles.description: Open the facility with continuously moving parcels and routing logic.

environments.title: Parcel Triage
environments.summary: Drive a humanoid station worker, operate conveyors, and route spawned parcels into a sorting hopper.
environments.description: Parcel Triage is a hands-on station environment: the robot can locomote or work from a fixed stance while the operator controls waist, arms, wrists, grippers, belts, and parcel spawning. It is the most direct sandbox for studying the control surface around a complete sorting task.

status.label: Interactive station
status.detail: Station interaction and parcel flow are available; locomotion is retained as an explicit prototype profile.

profiles.description: Operate the upper body, grippers, belts, and parcel flow.

environments.title: Quarry Loading
environments.summary: Inspect a scanned quarry, excavator, settled muck pile, and truck-loading geometry at full scene scale.
environments.description: Quarry Loading combines reconstructed site geometry with articulated heavy equipment and physically settled rocks. The current default profile is deliberately an inspection environment: it exposes the authored world and physics state while the complete autonomous dig/load cycle remains a documented engineering frontier.

status.label: Inspection scene
status.detail: The quarry, machine, truck, colliders, and settled muck are inspectable; full task execution is not claimed by this profile.

profiles.label: Inspect quarry scene
profiles.description: Open the saved scanned-muck scene for orbit, play, and collider inspection.

controls.action: Play to re-cook or re-settle physics
